qualifying_org_definition: Not explicitly defined in public-facing DSS materials; no pre-approved list or MOU requirement for volunteer sites found. DSS references the United Way as a resource for finding volunteer opportunities but does not restrict volunteer work to specific organization types. Federal 501(c)(3)/c(4)/government/place-of-worship standard presumed to apply.
verification_instrument: Supervisor-signed monthly log maintained by the volunteer site (showing hours with totals and supervisor signature); DSS may also request a generic 'DSS verification page' submitted by the participant at reporting and may contact the site by phone. No dedicated CT volunteer verification form number identified. W-1460 is for exemption requests only. Upload of proof via MyDSS portal is the standard reporting channel.
accepts_outofstate_detail: CT DSS public-facing ABAWD materials (portal.ct.gov/dss/snap/able-bodied-adults-without-dependents-or-abawds/abawd-work-rules and the H.R.1 changes page) impose no geographic restriction on volunteer site location. No in-state-only requirement or out-of-state exclusion was found across CT policy pages, practitioner resources (somedoing.com), or targeted searches. Federal default — any qualifying nonprofit regardless of state of incorporation — is presumed to apply.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. CT's prior statewide ABAWD waiver ended and ABAWD enforcement resumed November 2, 2025 under H.R.1 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act). No county-level or sub-state waivers were identified for 2026. FNA/USDA waiver pages (fna.usda.gov/snap/abawd/waivers/2025-2029) were inaccessible during research but CT DSS materials confirm uniform statewide enforcement.
submission_deadline: by the 10th day of the month after the month the change occurs (per CT DSS 'Changes you need to report' guidance)
notes:
Connecticut explicitly lists volunteer work (80 hrs/month) as a standalone ABAWD-qualifying activity with no evidence of mandatory pre-steps such as EDP, provider registration, MOU, or county assignment. Verification is via a supervisor-signed monthly log from the volunteer site; DSS may additionally request a generic 'DSS verification page' at reporting, but this is a post-hoc reporting instrument, not a pre-clearance requirement. Confidence is medium rather than high because: (1) no named CT volunteer verification form was found, (2) qualifying organization types are not enumerated in public materials, and (3) DSS policy manual was not accessible in machine-readable form to confirm absence of hoops definitively.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial adversarial research conducted across four fresh search angles and six direct page fetches to attempt refutation of the Tier 1 classification. Findings are consistently negative for any hidden blocking step: (1) No work-registration or initial-enrollment gate: Multiple CT DSS pages confirm that volunteer work (≥80 hrs/month) is a direct qualifying activity — participants self-report to DSS at application, renewal, or change via MyDSS upload, with no mandatory case manager intake, orientation, or employment development plan (EDP) required before hours begin to count. (2) No mandatory E&T enrollment: The CT SNAP E&T program (CTPathways, run with CT State Community College) is explicitly voluntary — the portaldir.ct.gov page states 'SNAP participants are not disqualified for failure to cooperate with E&T requirements because they are volunteers,' confirming E&T enrollment is not a gate to the general volunteer lane. (3) No approved site list found: Three separate searches and page fetches found no CT pre-approved volunteer organization list, no MOU or contract requirement for hosting sites, and no site-registration requirement. The DSS references United Way as a resource to find volunteer opportunities but imposes no site pre-clearance. (4) Verification instrument confirmed: somedoing.com (a practitioner-focused resource that surveyed state-by-state procedures) confirms CT uses a supervisor-signed monthly log plus an optional DSS verification page at reporting; DSS may call the site to confirm — all post-hoc, not pre-clearance. (5) ABAWD enforcement confirmed active statewide: H.R.1 ended CT's statewide waiver; enforcement began December 1, 2025 with age range expanded to 18–64. (6) Remote/online stance remains unresolved: No CT source addresses whether remote volunteer work qualifies; this field stays 'unknown'. Confidence raised to 'high' because: the Tier 1 classification is confirmed by direct policy-page language (no prestep, no site list, self-reported via monthly log), CT SNAP E&T is confirmed voluntary and non-gating, and the adversarial search found zero counter-evidence. The only remaining gap — qualifying org definition and remote stance — does not affect the tier rating because the lane itself is unambiguously open without prestep or site pre-approval.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours/formula: CT DSS explicitly states '80 hours' per month for volunteer work on both the work-rules page and the H.R.1 changes page — federal_80 formula confirmed. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: No CT source imposes an in-state-only restriction on volunteer organizations; field set to 'silent' (federal default of any qualifying nonprofit presumed). Targeted search for CT in-state nonprofit requirement for ABAWD found no restricting language. (3) Geographic waivers: CT's prior statewide waiver ended; enforcement is uniform statewide as of November 2, 2025. FNA waiver page timed out but CT DSS materials confirm no sub-state waivers apply. (4) Submission UX: CT DSS 'Changes you need to report' page confirms deadline of 10th day of following month; MyDSS upload (online_portal), mail to DSS Scanning Center, and in-person at DSS Resource Center (paper_county_office) are all accepted. Reporting cadence is monthly. (5) Form fields: No named CT volunteer verification form was found; default supervisor-signed log baseline applied.
